zh.Chilham, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆
zh.Chilham Castle, Kent, England: 英國肯特爾哈姆 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham於1220年出生在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆 卡素時，他的父親Baron Richard Fitzroy30歲，母親Rohese de Dover11217歲。他於大約1245年與Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu在英國英格蘭肯特多福結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1270年8月9日在突尼西亞تونس卡薩享年50歲。

zh-hans.Chilham, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆
zh-hans.Chilham Castle, Kent, England: 英国肯特爾哈姆 卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham于1220年出生在英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆 卡素时，他的父亲Baron Richard Fitzroy30岁，母亲Rohese de Dover11217岁。他于大约1245年与Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu在英国英格兰肯特多福结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1270年8月9日在突尼西亚تونس卡薩享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dover
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from either the port of Dover in Kent, named from the river on which it stands, Celtic dubrās meaning ‘the waters’ (which has become modern Welsh dwfr ‘water’), or Dover in Wigan, Lancashire.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhuidhir or Mac Duibhidhir; see Dwyer .
Americanized form of German   Dauer   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Dwyer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Parnell, Liam, Brigid, Cormac, Oonagh, Patrick Michael.

Irish (southern Ireland): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhuidhir, a variant of Ó Duibhidhir (see Diver ), or Mac Duibhidhir ‘descendant of Duibhuidhir’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘dark, black’ + odhar ‘sallow, tawny’.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Chilham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish:  see  Chisholm  .

variants.name: Chisholm
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Chisholme near Hawick in southern Scotland, which derives its name from Old English c̄se, cēse ‘cheese’ (from Latin caseus) + holm ‘piece of dry land in a fen’ and refers to a waterside meadow good for dairy farming and hence for producing cheeses. In the 14th century members of this family migrated to the Highlands, settling in Strathglass, where their name was Gaelicized as Siosal.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham was born in 1220, in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Baron Richard Fitzroy, was 30 and his mother, Rohese de Dover, was 11217. He married Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu about 1245, in Dover, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 9 August 1270, in Carthage, Tunis, Tunisia, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham est né en 1220, à Chilham Castle, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Richard Fitzroy, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Rohese de Dover, avait 11217 ans. Il a épousé Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu environ 1245, à Dover, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 9 août 1270, à Carthage, Tunis, Tunisie, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham 1220 in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baron Richard Fitzroy 30 und seine Mutter Rohese de Dover 11217. Er hat um 1245 in Dover, Kent, England, United Kingdom Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 9. August 1270 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Carthage, Tunis, Tunesien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham è nato il nel 1220 nel Chilham Castle, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Baron Richard Fitzroy, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Rohese de Dover, aveva 11217 anni. Lui sposato Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu circa 1245, nel Dover, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 9 agosto 1270, nel Carthage, Tunis, Tunisia, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilhamが1220年にイギリスイギリスケントチルハム カストルで生まれた時，父Baron Richard Fitzroyは30歳，母Rohese de Doverは11217歳であった。約1245年にイギリスイギリスケントドバーでMatilda de Angus Countess of Anguと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1270年8月9日にチュニジアتونسカーサッジで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham(이)가 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baron Richard Fitzroy(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Rohese de Dover(은)는 11217세였다. 그는 대략 1245년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 도버에서 Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년 8월 9일 튀니지 تونس 카스아지에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham nasceu em 1220, em Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Rohese de Dover, tinha 11217 anos. Ele casou-se com Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu aproximadamente 1245, em Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de agosto de 1270, em Carthage, Tunis, Tunísia, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham родился в 1220 г. в местности чилхам кастл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Baron Richard Fitzroy было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Rohese de Dover, 11217. Он женился на женщине по имени Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu около 1245 г. в местности довир, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 9 августа 1270 г. в местности картадж, تونس, Тунис в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Dover - Baron of Chilham nació en 1220, en Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baron Richard Fitzroy, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Rohese de Dover, tenía 11217 años. Se casó con Matilda de Angus Countess of Angu aproximadamente 1245, en Dover, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1270, en Carthage, Tunis, Túnez, a la edad de 50 años.
